colorTokensLight.name: --ob-background
colorTokensLight.usage: App canvas. Warm off-white (paper, not clinical #FFF) echoing the editorial references; keeps lime accent from vibrating.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-surface
colorTokensLight.usage: Primary card / sheet fill sitting on the warm canvas. The large rounded cards from references.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-surface-elevated
colorTokensLight.usage: Modals, popovers, floating pill nav — same fill but carries a stronger shadow tier for separation.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-surface-sunken
colorTokensLight.usage: Inset wells: search fields, segmented-control tracks, code blocks, progress troughs.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-surface-inverse
colorTokensLight.usage: The signature near-black panel used for stat strips / footers in light mode (see references).

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-border
colorTokensLight.usage: Default hairline divider/card outline. 1px, low contrast, calm.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-border-strong
colorTokensLight.usage: Input borders, emphasized dividers, hover outlines needing presence.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-text-primary
colorTokensLight.usage: Headlines and body. 15.8:1 on background — AAA.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-text-secondary
colorTokensLight.usage: Supporting copy, card descriptions. 8.0:1 — AAA.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-text-tertiary
colorTokensLight.usage: Captions, metadata, placeholders, disabled labels. 4.6:1 — AA.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-text-on-inverse
colorTokensLight.usage: Text on --ob-surface-inverse near-black panels.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-accent
colorTokensLight.usage: THE lime. Sparing: one focal pop per view — selected node, key progress fill, hero highlight, active mastery ring. Never a flood, never large body text.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-accent-hover
colorTokensLight.usage: Hover/active state of accent-filled controls.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-accent-foreground
colorTokensLight.usage: Text/icons ON the lime. Deep green-black gives 11.6:1 on #C0FE6F — AAA. (White text on lime FAILS, so accent is never a primary text-bearing button by default.)

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-accent-subtle
colorTokensLight.usage: Tinted lime wash for selected rows, success-ish 'mastered' chips, accent card backgrounds — carries the green without shouting.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-accent-ring
colorTokensLight.usage: Border/ring derivative of lime for outlining accent-subtle surfaces (more visible than the wash alone).

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-cta
colorTokensLight.usage: Primary CTA = black pill in light mode (per DNA). Lime is reserved as the rare accent, not the default button.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-cta-foreground
colorTokensLight.usage: Text on the black pill CTA.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-success
colorTokensLight.usage: Correct answer / completed / mastery. Distinct from lime so 'system success' never collides with 'brand accent'. 4.7:1 on bg — AA.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-success-subtle
colorTokensLight.usage: Success row/toast background.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-warning
colorTokensLight.usage: 'Review soon', low-confidence, partial recall. Amber leans dark-yellow for colorblind separability from success-green.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-warning-subtle
colorTokensLight.usage: Warning background fill.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-danger
colorTokensLight.usage: Incorrect, destructive, expired. Paired ALWAYS with an icon/shape, never color alone.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-danger-subtle
colorTokensLight.usage: Danger background fill.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-info
colorTokensLight.usage: Neutral system info / 'AI is thinking' / hints. Blue completes a colorblind-safe quad with green/amber/red.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-info-subtle
colorTokensLight.usage: Info background fill.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-focus-ring
colorTokensLight.usage: Keyboard focus halo. Deliberately blue (not lime) so focus is unmistakable for low-vision/colorblind users and never confused with the brand accent. 3px outline + 2px offset.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-overlay
colorTokensLight.usage: Scrim behind modals/sheets.

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-glass-fill
colorTokensLight.usage: Glassmorphic pill nav / spatial panels: pair with backdrop-blur(28px) saturate(160%).

colorTokensLight.name: --ob-glass-border
colorTokensLight.usage: Top/inner highlight edge for glass surfaces.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-background
colorTokensDark.usage: Dark app canvas — one step below the brand base so cards (#242424/#2C2C2C) lift off it. Avoids pure black for reduced eye strain (weak-eyesight market).

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-surface
colorTokensDark.usage: Primary card fill — the official OpenBook dark base.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-surface-elevated
colorTokensDark.usage: Modals, popovers, floating pill nav — the second official dark base, used as the elevated tier.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-surface-sunken
colorTokensDark.usage: Inset wells: search, segmented tracks, code blocks, progress troughs.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-surface-inverse
colorTokensDark.usage: Rare light panel inside dark mode for high-emphasis moments (mirror of the light-mode black panel).

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-border
colorTokensDark.usage: Default hairline on dark. Borders do more separation work than shadow in dark mode.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-border-strong
colorTokensDark.usage: Input borders, hover outlines, emphasized dividers.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-text-primary
colorTokensDark.usage: Headlines/body. Slightly warm off-white, 14.9:1 on #242424 — AAA. Pure #FFF avoided to cut halation.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-text-secondary
colorTokensDark.usage: Supporting copy. 7.4:1 — AAA.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-text-tertiary
colorTokensDark.usage: Captions, metadata, placeholders. 4.6:1 — AA.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-text-on-inverse
colorTokensDark.usage: Text on the rare light --ob-surface-inverse panel.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-accent
colorTokensDark.usage: Same lime, equally sparing. On dark it reads as a glowing focal pop — perfect for the active concept node / mastery ring.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-accent-hover
colorTokensDark.usage: Hover lightens slightly on dark for tactile lift.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-accent-foreground
colorTokensDark.usage: Deep green-black text on lime — 11.6:1, identical pairing across modes for consistency.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-accent-subtle
colorTokensDark.usage: Desaturated lime-tinted surface for selected rows / 'mastered' chips on dark — the green whisper, not a flood.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-accent-ring
colorTokensDark.usage: Border/ring for accent-subtle surfaces on dark.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-cta
colorTokensDark.usage: Primary CTA in dark mode = light/white pill (inverse of light-mode black pill), keeping lime reserved as accent.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-cta-foreground
colorTokensDark.usage: Text on the light pill CTA in dark mode.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-success
colorTokensDark.usage: Lightened for dark-bg legibility. 5.9:1 on #242424 — AA. Held apart from lime hue.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-success-subtle
colorTokensDark.usage: Success row/toast background on dark.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-warning
colorTokensDark.usage: Amber tuned up for dark. 6.8:1 — AA. Colorblind-separated from success.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-warning-subtle
colorTokensDark.usage: Warning background on dark.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-danger
colorTokensDark.usage: Lightened red for dark legibility, 5.4:1 — AA. Always icon-paired.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-danger-subtle
colorTokensDark.usage: Danger background on dark.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-info
colorTokensDark.usage: Info/hint blue for dark, 6.1:1 — AA. Completes colorblind-safe quad.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-info-subtle
colorTokensDark.usage: Info background on dark.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-focus-ring
colorTokensDark.usage: Blue keyboard-focus halo on dark — never lime, unmistakable for accessibility. 3px outline + 2px offset.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-overlay
colorTokensDark.usage: Scrim behind modals/sheets on dark.

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-glass-fill
colorTokensDark.usage: Glass pill nav / spatial panels on dark: backdrop-blur(28px) saturate(140%).

colorTokensDark.name: --ob-glass-border
colorTokensDark.usage: Subtle top highlight edge for dark glass.

typographyScale.name: display-2xl
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Display Bold. Hero headlines ('Bring your chaos to clarity' energy). Tight tracking is the signature. Desktop only; clamps down on mobile.

typographyScale.name: display-xl
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Display Bold. Secondary hero / big section openers.

typographyScale.name: display-lg
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Display Bold/Semibold. Page titles, marquee numbers in stat strips.

typographyScale.name: title-1
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Display Semibold. Major section headings ('My Learning Plan').

typographyScale.name: title-2
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Display Semibold. Card cluster headings, modal titles.

typographyScale.name: title-3
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Display Semibold. Card titles ('Medical Terminology'), concept-node labels.

typographyScale.name: headline
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Text Semibold. Emphasized body lead-ins, list-item titles, active tab.

typographyScale.name: body-lg
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Text Regular. Long-form reading / lesson content. 17px is the Apple comfortable-reading baseline.

typographyScale.name: body
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Text Regular. Default UI body, card descriptions, paragraph copy.

typographyScale.name: callout
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Text Medium. Buttons, pill nav labels, chips, segmented controls.

typographyScale.name: caption
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Text Regular. Metadata, helper text, timestamps, tooltip body.

typographyScale.name: caption-sm
typographyScale.usage: SF Pro Text Semibold, UPPERCASE. Eyebrow/overline labels, section kickers, badge text.

typographyScale.name: mono
typographyScale.usage: SF Mono / ui-monospace. Code blocks, .ipynb cells, citation IDs, tabular numerals (use font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums).

typographyScale.name: mono-numeral
typographyScale.usage: SF Mono tabular-nums. Stat counters, timers, streak counts, progress %, mastery scores — so digits don't jitter while animating.

spacingAndRadii:
SPACING — 4px base grid, named on an Apple-style geometric-ish ramp. Tokens (--ob-space-*): 0=0, px=1, 0.5=2, 1=4, 1.5=6, 2=8, 3=12, 4=16, 5=20, 6=24, 8=32, 10=40, 12=48, 16=64, 20=80, 24=96, 32=128, 40=160. Component rules: chip/pill padding 6/12, button 10/18, input 12/14, card padding 24 (mobile) / 28-32 (desktop), card-to-card gap 16-24, section vertical rhythm 96-128, content max-width 1200 (app) / 1120 (marketing prose 680). Touch targets >=44x44 always (HIG). Generous whitespace is a brand requirement — when unsure, go one step larger.

RADII (--ob-radius-*): xs=6 (chips, badges, inline code), sm=10 (inputs, small buttons, segmented items), md=14 (buttons, tooltips, toasts), lg=20 (cards, list rows), xl=28 (large feature cards, modals, the big rounded cards in references), 2xl=36 (hero panels, sheets), pill=999 (pill nav, pill CTAs, avatars, status dots), full=9999. Nesting rule: child radius = parent radius − padding, so concentric corners stay optically parallel (e.g. 28px card with 16px inset image -> ~12px image radius). Focus rings and shadows follow the element's own radius.
shadowsAndElevation:
Six-tier elevation. Soft, low-spread, multi-layer, near-neutral (a hair warm in light, pure-dark in dark) — Apple-grade, never harsh. Light mode leans on shadow; dark mode leans on borders + a faint top inner-highlight since shadows barely read on #1E1E1E.

LIGHT (--ob-shadow-*):
  e0 (flush): none — flat list rows on surface.
  e1 (raised): 0 1px 2px rgba(20,18,14,.05), 0 1px 1px rgba(20,18,14,.03) — chips, inputs at rest.
  e2 (card): 0 2px 4px rgba(20,18,14,.05), 0 6px 14px rgba(20,18,14,.06) — the default large rounded card.
  e3 (floating): 0 4px 8px rgba(20,18,14,.06), 0 14px 30px rgba(20,18,14,.09) — floating pill nav, hover-lifted card, popovers.
  e4 (overlay): 0 8px 16px rgba(20,18,14,.08), 0 28px 60px rgba(20,18,14,.14) — modals, sheets, command palette.
  e-accent (focus glow): 0 0 0 4px rgba(192,254,111,.30) — the ONE place lime is allowed as a glow, around an active concept node / selected mastery card.

DARK (--ob-shadow-*):
  e1: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.40) + inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.04).
  e2: 0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,.45), 0 8px 20px rgba(0,0,0,.40) + inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.05).
  e3: 0 6px 16px rgba(0,0,0,.50), 0 18px 40px rgba(0,0,0,.50) + inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.06).
  e4: 0 10px 24px rgba(0,0,0,.55), 0 32px 70px rgba(0,0,0,.62) + inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.06).
  e-accent: 0 0 0 4px rgba(192,254,111,.22), 0 0 24px rgba(192,254,111,.18) (a true glow, since dark backgrounds carry it).

GLASS elevation (premium/spatial surfaces): background var(--ob-glass-fill); backdrop-filter blur(28px) saturate(160%); 1px var(--ob-glass-border) top edge; plus e3 beneath. Reserved for pill nav and the optional spatial/visionOS-style panels seen in references. Hairline borders (var(--ob-border)) accompany e1–e2 cards in both modes for crisp edges on busy backgrounds.
motionLanguage:
Philosophy: motion clarifies state change, it never decorates. Short, eased, interruptible — Apple restraint. Everything respects prefers-reduced-motion.

DURATIONS (--ob-dur-*): instant 80ms (token/checkbox toggles), fast 140ms (hover, pressed, focus-ring), base 220ms (most enter/exit, tab/segment slide), slow 320ms (modal/sheet, accordion, route cross-fade), slowest 480ms (page hero stagger, concept-graph relayout).

EASINGS (--ob-ease-*):
  standard cubic-bezier(.2,0,0,1) — default in/out, the 'iOS' feel.
  entrance (decelerate) cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1) — elements arriving (sheets up, popovers in).
  exit (accelerate) cubic-bezier(.4,0,1,1) — elements leaving.
  spring (overshoot) cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1) — used ONLY for celebratory beats (mastery achieved, correct answer, streak +1); never on routine UI.

SIGNATURE TRANSITIONS:
  • Hover-lift on cards: translateY(-2px) + shadow e2->e3 over fast/standard. Cursor never feels sticky.
  • Press: scale .98 + shadow drop over instant; releases on standard. Pills/buttons/cards.
  • Floating pill nav: active item is a sliding lime-or-black 'puck' that animates left/right via spring-lite (.34,1.1,.5,1) at base — the layout-id transition (framer-motion layoutId) so the indicator glides between tabs.
  • Concept-graph: nodes fade+scale-in (from .96) staggered 40ms along edges as connections 'draw' (SVG stroke-dashoffset 0->full) over slowest — the learning-native reveal.
  • Streaming AI text: tokens fade-in opacity 0->1 over 120ms each, plus a 2px lime caret that pulses (opacity 1<->.3, 900ms) while generating; caret removed on stop.
  • Mastery ring fill: SVG circle stroke-dashoffset eases over slow with the spring easing on the final 10% for a satisfying 'lock-in'.

MICRO-INTERACTIONS:
  • Correct answer: success-subtle flash + a single 8px lime confetti burst (≤6 particles) + spring scale 1->1.04->1. Wrong: 2-cycle 6px horizontal shake over 180ms (paired with danger color + ✕ icon, never color alone).
  • Toast: slides in from bottom-right translateY(16px)+fade entrance, auto-dismiss slides out exit.
  • Skeleton/loading: shimmer is a slow (1400ms) gradient sweep at 8% contrast — calm, not strobing.
  • Magnifier lens: scales in from .9 with entrance easing, tracks cursor with no transition (1:1) so it never lags.

REDUCED MOTION (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce OR ADHD/[data-reduced-motion]): all translate/scale/spring collapse to opacity cross-fades capped at 120ms; confetti, shake, caret-pulse, shimmer sweep, and graph stroke-draw are disabled (graph nodes simply appear); mastery ring snaps to value. Layout-id puck jumps instantly. We honor it via a global @media block AND a JS-readable data attribute so the ADHD focus mode can force it independently of OS settings.
accessibilitySystem:
Accessibility is a first-class, always-on layer (secondary market: ADHD, Dyslexia, Color Blindness, Weak Eyesight). A floating 'Accessibility' control (bottom-left, glass pill) opens a sheet toggling four independent modes, each a data-attribute on <html> so they compose freely and persist to Supabase user prefs.

1) HOVER MAGNIFIER / BOLD TOOL (the signature accessibility flourish). A togglable 'Reading Lens' with two interchangeable modes:
   • Magnify: a circular lens (default 160px, scroll-to-resize 96–280px) follows the cursor and renders a 1.8x–3x zoom (slider) of whatever text/UI is under it. Implemented as a fixed overlay that clones the hovered region via CSS transform scale on a duplicated layer (or background-image of the element box), clipped to a circle with a 1px border + e3 shadow + faint lime ring. Crucially it is an OVERLAY — underlying layout never reflows, so the design is preserved pixel-for-pixel.
   • Bold/Boost: instead of zooming, the hovered text node gets font-weight bumped +200 and letter-spacing +0.01em transiently (class toggled on :hover target) so any text anywhere can be 'boosted' for legibility WITHOUT changing size or layout (we reserve space by transitioning weight only). Works on body, captions, code.
   Keyboard: hold Alt to summon the lens at caret; arrow keys nudge. Disabled automatically inside text inputs. Honors reduced-motion (lens tracks without easing regardless).

2) DYSLEXIA MODE ([data-dyslexia='on']): swaps display+text families to self-hosted OpenDyslexic (SIL-licensed); increases letter-spacing to 0.035em, word-spacing to 0.12em, line-height +0.2, paragraph max-width to ~66ch; left-aligns (never justified); adds an optional tinted reading background (--ob-surface warm cream) and an optional reading-ruler (a translucent horizontal band that follows the line under the cursor). Mono/code stays mono. All purely typographic — zero layout breakage.

3) ADHD FOCUS MODE ([data-focus='on']): dims everything except the active learning card to 35% opacity + slight blur (a 'spotlight' on the current task); hides non-essential chrome (sidebars collapse, decorative textures off); forces reduced-motion; surfaces a single 'Next step' affordance; optional Pomodoro ring in the nav. Reduces choice and visual noise — the platform's active-learning thesis benefits directly. One concept on screen at a time.

4) COLORBLIND-SAFE (default-on philosophy, with [data-cvd='deut|prot|trit'] fine-tuning): status is NEVER conveyed by hue alone — every state carries an icon AND/OR shape AND/OR text (✓ success, ! warning, ✕ danger, i info). The semantic quad is chosen for separability across deuter/protan/tritan: green #2E8B57, amber #B7791F, red #D14343, blue #3F6FE0 differ in lightness as well as hue. The lime brand accent is decorative/focal only — it never alone encodes meaning. Concept-graph edges use line style (solid=prereq, dashed=related) plus color. Focus ring is blue, distinct from lime. CVD sub-modes shift the amber/green pair apart further (e.g. deut: nudge success toward teal, warning toward orange) via a token override layer.

CROSS-CUTTING: WCAG-AA minimum on all text/UI (most pairings hit AAA — see color usage notes); visible 3px blue focus ring + 2px offset on every interactive element; full keyboard operability and logical tab order; :focus-visible only (no mouse-focus rings); prefers-reduced-motion + prefers-contrast honored; min 44px touch targets; respects OS reduce-transparency by dropping glass to solid surfaces; all icons that carry meaning get aria-labels; live regions announce AI streaming completion and quiz results.

componentInventory.name: Button — Primary (Pill CTA)
componentInventory.purpose: Black pill in light / white pill in dark (--ob-cta). Rounded-full, callout type, press-scale .98, e1->e2 on hover. The hero/primary action. Lime is NOT the default primary (reserved as accent).

componentInventory.name: Button — Accent
componentInventory.purpose: Sparingly: lime fill (--ob-accent) with --ob-accent-foreground deep-green text. One per view max — 'Start studying', 'Mark mastered'. Carries e-accent glow on focus.

componentInventory.name: Button — Secondary / Outline / Ghost
componentInventory.purpose: Secondary = surface fill + border-strong ('Talk to sales' style). Ghost = transparent, text-secondary, hover surface-sunken. Destructive = danger text + icon.

componentInventory.name: Icon Button
componentInventory.purpose: 44px hit area, circular or md radius, used in toolbars, card overflow (…), chat send (dark circle ↑ from references).

componentInventory.name: Floating Pill Nav
componentInventory.purpose: Glassmorphic rounded-full bar, center-docked. Sliding active 'puck' (framer-motion layoutId). Wordmark 'OpenBook' at left. The signature top navigation.

componentInventory.name: Sidebar / Rail
componentInventory.purpose: Icon rail (left) + collapsible labeled nav. Collapses in ADHD focus mode. Concept/library tree lives here.

componentInventory.name: Card — Standard
componentInventory.purpose: Surface, radius-xl, e2, 24-32 padding. The workhorse container (lesson cards 'Medical Terminology').

componentInventory.name: Card — Feature / Stat
componentInventory.purpose: Large radius-xl card, often dark inverse panel holding stat strip (50,000+ / 92%). Big mono-numeral figures + caption labels.

componentInventory.name: Card — Concept Node
componentInventory.purpose: Learning-native node in the connected graph: title-3 label, mastery ring, prereq edges. Active node = accent border + e-accent glow. The DNA differentiator.

componentInventory.name: Input / Textarea
componentInventory.purpose: Surface-sunken or surface fill, border-strong, radius-sm, 12/14 padding, blue focus-ring. Includes the rounded 'Ask anything…' chat composer with attach + send.

componentInventory.name: Search Field
componentInventory.purpose: Sunken pill with leading magnifier icon; ⌘K opens command palette variant.

componentInventory.name: Select / Dropdown / Combobox
componentInventory.purpose: Trigger styled like input; popover menu (surface-elevated, e4) with checkable items; used for AI model picker (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) and 'AI Module' from references.

componentInventory.name: Segmented Control
componentInventory.purpose: Sunken track, sliding indicator puck (Recent/Favorite, Monthly/Annual). callout type.

componentInventory.name: Tabs
componentInventory.purpose: Underline or pill tabs with animated active indicator; headline weight on active.

componentInventory.name: Chip / Tag / Badge
componentInventory.purpose: radius-xs, callout-sm. Status chips ('Completed' ✓, 'Upcoming'), topic tags ('Auto-sorts'), 'New' badge (success-subtle). Always icon+text for status.

componentInventory.name: Checkbox / Radio / Toggle
componentInventory.purpose: Custom; checked uses success or accent with a ✓; toggle is iOS-style pill. instant duration.

componentInventory.name: Tooltip
componentInventory.purpose: surface-elevated, radius-md, e3, caption type, 8px offset, 120ms fade. Arrow optional. Dismissible, keyboard-reachable.

componentInventory.name: Popover / Menu
componentInventory.purpose: surface-elevated, radius-lg, e4, sectioned items; right-click and overflow menus.

componentInventory.name: Modal / Dialog
componentInventory.purpose: Centered, radius-xl, e4, scrim overlay, entrance/exit easings, focus-trapped, Esc to close.

componentInventory.name: Sheet / Drawer
componentInventory.purpose: Bottom (mobile) / side (desktop) panel, radius-2xl top, slides via entrance easing. Hosts Accessibility settings, filters, citations.

componentInventory.name: Toast / Snackbar
componentInventory.purpose: Bottom-right stack, radius-md, e3, status icon + message + action; auto-dismiss with pause-on-hover; live-region announced.

componentInventory.name: Banner / Inline Alert
componentInventory.purpose: Full-width info/warning/danger with subtle bg + icon; used for 'review due' nudges.

componentInventory.name: Progress — Bar & Ring
componentInventory.purpose: Mastery ring (SVG, accent or success fill, mono-numeral center) and linear progress (sunken trough + fill). Animated per motion spec.

componentInventory.name: Avatar / Avatar Stack
componentInventory.purpose: Pill-round, status dot, overlap stack (study-group / collaborators from references).

componentInventory.name: Accordion / Disclosure
componentInventory.purpose: slow ease height; for outlines, FAQ, expandable lesson sections.

componentInventory.name: Skeleton / Loading
componentInventory.purpose: surface-sunken blocks with calm 1400ms shimmer; respects reduced-motion (static).

componentInventory.name: Empty State
componentInventory.purpose: Centered illustration slot + title-2 + body + primary action; for new notebooks/libraries.

componentInventory.name: Command Palette (⌘K)
componentInventory.purpose: Overlay search with grouped actions/navigation/AI commands; the power-user spine.

componentInventory.name: Code Block / Notebook Cell
componentInventory.purpose: surface-sunken, mono type, language tag, copy button; renders .ipynb/.md/code uploads with citation anchors.

componentInventory.name: Chat / Message Bubble
componentInventory.purpose: User bubble (info-subtle, right) vs AI bubble (surface, left) with streaming caret, copy/👍/👎 actions, inline citations — the active-learning dialogue surface.

componentInventory.name: Quiz / Flashcard
componentInventory.purpose: Flip card (3D transform), MCQ options with correct/incorrect micro-interactions, confidence rating — core active-recall components.

componentInventory.name: Upload / Dropzone
componentInventory.purpose: Dashed border-strong zone, drag-active accent ring, file-type chips (PDF/DOCX/PPTX/YouTube/GitHub), progress per file.

componentInventory.name: Accessibility Control & Magnifier Lens
componentInventory.purpose: Floating glass pill (bottom-left) opening the modes sheet; the cursor-following Reading Lens overlay (magnify/bold) is its always-available companion.

componentInventory.name: Reading Ruler
componentInventory.purpose: Dyslexia-mode translucent line-highlight band that follows the active reading line.

componentInventory.name: Halftone/Dotted Texture & Spatial Panel
componentInventory.purpose: Decorative dotted/halftone background layer and optional glass spatial panel for premium hero/empty states (DNA), disabled in ADHD focus & reduce-transparency.

summaryMarkdown:
## OpenBook Design System — Rationale

A complete, production-ready token set for **OpenBook**, an AI **active-learning** platform. It is engineered for the existing stack found in the repo (**Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind 3.4 + framer-motion + Supabase**, CSS-variable theming already present in `tailwind.config.ts`/`globals.css`). The system encodes the Apple-HIG philosophy and the curated **design DNA** (tight-tracked bold display headlines, calm editorial whitespace, large rounded soft-shadow cards, floating glass pill nav, black/white pill CTAs, dotted-halftone texture, **one sparing lime pop**, connected concept nodes).

### The core idea: lime is a scalpel, not a paintbrush
`#C0FE6F` is gorgeous but high-luminance — flooding it kills contrast and cheapens the brand. So the accent is **structurally reserved**: primary CTAs are the **black pill (light) / white pill (dark)** seen in the references; brand lime appears **once per view** as the focal beat (active concept node, mastery ring, a single highlighted word, the correct-answer burst). System status uses a **separate, colorblind-safe quad** (green/amber/red/blue) so "brand accent" never collides with "success," and the **focus ring is blue** — unmistakable and never confused with lime.

### Dual mode, both first-class
| | Light | Dark |
|---|---|---|
| Canvas | warm paper `#F7F6F3` | `#1E1E1E` (one step under brand base) |
| Card | `#FFFFFF` | `#242424` (official base) |
| Elevated | `#FFFFFF` + stronger shadow | `#2C2C2C` (official base) |
| Primary CTA | black pill `#1A1A1A` | white pill `#F4F3EF` |
| Text primary | `#1A1A1A` (15.8:1) | `#F4F3EF` (14.9:1) |

Off-white/off-black (not `#FFF`/`#000`) reduce halation for the **weak-eyesight** market; dark mode leans on **borders + inner top-highlight** because shadows barely read on near-black, while light mode leans on **soft multi-layer shadows**.

### Contrast & colorblindness (WCAG-AA, mostly AAA)
Every text token is annotated with its measured ratio in the color tokens. Body/secondary text hits **AAA**; tertiary/status hit **AA**. Status is **never hue-only** — always icon + shape + text — and the semantic quad is chosen to separate by *lightness* as well as hue across deuter/protan/tritan, with `[data-cvd]` sub-mode overrides.

### Typography — SF Pro, done correctly
- **SF Pro Display** (Semibold/Bold) for `title-1`↑ and all `display-*`, with the **tight negative tracking** that is the brand's signature (down to −0.035em at 76px). **SF Pro Text** for `headline`↓. **SF Mono / ui-monospace** for code and **tabular numerals** (counters/timers/scores don't jitter while animating).
- We **do not ship SF Pro woff** (Apple-licensed). Strategy: `@font-face local()` bridge → real SF Pro on Apple devices, with `-apple-system`/`system-ui` fallback for everyone else. **No Inter/Roboto** (the existing Geist fonts are dropped) — that would violate the brand. A self-hosted **OpenDyslexic** (SIL-licensed, shippable) powers the dyslexia toggle via `[data-dyslexia]` without touching layout.

### Spacing, radii, elevation, motion
4px grid; concentric-corner radii (xs 6 → 2xl 36 → pill); six-tier soft elevation with a single sanctioned **lime focus-glow** (`e-accent`); glassmorphic tier for the pill nav/spatial panels. Motion is **short, eased, interruptible** (80–480ms, iOS-style `cubic-bezier(.2,0,0,1)`), with spring **only** for celebratory beats (mastery, correct answer, streak). Signature moments: sliding pill-nav puck (`layoutId`), concept-graph stroke-draw reveal, streaming-AI fade-in with a pulsing lime caret. Full **`prefers-reduced-motion`** story (collapses to ≤120ms cross-fades; disables confetti/shake/shimmer).

### Accessibility as a product pillar (not an afterthought)
Four composable modes on `<html>` data-attributes, persisted to Supabase:
1. **Reading Lens** — the signature flourish: a cursor-following **magnifier** (1.8–3×, scroll-to-resize) *or* **bold/boost** that thickens any hovered text — both implemented as **overlays / weight-only transitions so layout never reflows**.
2. **Dyslexia mode** — OpenDyslexic + looser tracking/leading + 66ch measure + reading ruler, purely typographic.
3. **ADHD focus mode** — spotlight the active learning card, dim/blur the rest, kill decorative texture, force reduced-motion, surface one "Next step." Directly serves the **active-learning thesis** (one concept at a time).
4. **Colorblind-safe** — always-on icon/shape encoding + `[data-cvd]` tuning + blue (non-lime) focus ring.

### Why this is "active learning," not a generator
The inventory includes **learning-native** components competitors lack: connected **concept-node graph** with mastery rings, **flashcard/quiz** with active-recall micro-interactions (confidence rating, correct/incorrect feedback), a **streaming chat tutor** with inline citations, and a **focus mode** that enforces single-task study. The tokens make the *interactive learning loop* — not one-shot output — feel premium, calm, and unmistakably Apple-grade.

### Build notes (wiring)
- Define every token as a CSS variable under `:root` (light) and `[data-theme='dark']` (dark) in `globals.css`; map semantic names in `tailwind.config.ts theme.extend.colors/boxShadow/borderRadius/fontFamily`.
- Set `--font-display/--font-text/--font-mono/--font-dyslexic` and reference via Tailwind `fontFamily`.
- Mode flags: `data-theme`, `data-dyslexia`, `data-focus`, `data-cvd`, `data-reduced-motion`, `data-lens` on `<html>` — composable and SSR-safe.
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`, `prefers-contrast`, and `prefers-reduced-transparency` (drop glass → solid).
